text:
Equipment: RIE

RIE Operational Manual

Back room

1. turn on “MAINS”

2. turn on “COOLING”

3. rotate the valve to left

6. Push green “ON” button

4. turn on both Compressor and N2 Valves

7. turn on small chiller, wait for 20 ℃

5. Clockwise to release “STOP” button

8. open software, Name: OPT

Password: OPT

9. ACCEPT all alerts

11. ATP is the temp of turbo pump

ACT is the temp of the machine controller

KEEP AN EYE ON THEM ALL THE TIME

10. Press the status button

13. Click “Cancel” as there is no wafer in it

12. Click “Evacuate”

13. After seeing “Base pressure reached”, STOP the evacuation

14. wait for turbo to reach 27000 rpm

15. “Stop” venting when the status shows “Finished”

14. Click “Venting”

18. Put sample on the stage(tape it if necessary)

16. switch to “CHAMBER UP”

17. Press both buttons together until the lid completely opens up

19. Turn the knob to “CHAMBER DOWN” and press both the hoist together until the lid completely closes

21. Click “OK” as sample is in it and wait for the screen to show “BASE PRESSURE REACHED”

20. Click “Evacuate”

22. turn on “AC LINE” SLOWLY and make sure the green light is on.

Equipment: RIE

23. If the green light doesn’t turn ON, DO NOT PLAY WITH THE AC LINE, go back of the RIE and one of the circuit breakers have been tripped.

24. Turn the breaker back ON

26. go to “Process”-“Recipe”

25. Make sure the AC ON is glowing now

27. Click “LOAD” and then select “PDMS” (or any recipe that you want) and “OK”

28. Click the recipe and then click “Edit Step”

29. make sure the flow rate, process time, power numbers are right

30. “Run” the recipe

31. check for “all tolerance ok” and look for plasma in the chamber

32. While the process is running, keep an eye for flow rate, forward power and plasma in the chamber

33. If you are running the process for a longer time, look out for the temperatures of ACT and ATP. If the ACT is showing high temp, be cautious and check for any warnings on the computer screen

34. After the process is finished, ACCEPT ALL THE YELLOW ALERTS and wait for the PROCESS CONTROL to show READY.

35. Go to “System” and choose “Pumping”

36. Turn off “AC LINE”… SLOWLY

37. “Stop” and then “Vent”

38. “Stop” the venting when it shows “Finished”

41. collect sample away from the stage (or leave it in if needed more time)

39. switch to “CHAMBER UP”

Equipment: RIE

40. Press both button until the lid totally opens

42. Switch to “CHAMBER DOWN” and press both hoists until the lid is completely closed.

Repeat the steps from 20 to 42 if you are repeating the process.

If you are done, follow the next steps to shut the machine down.

44. Click “Cancel” as there is no wafer in it

43. Click “Evacuate”

46. Go to “System”-“Service” then click the “Turbo pump” image and click YES to turn it off

45. Wait for the screen to show “Base pressure reached”

48. go to “System”-“Exit Service” and click YES to exit the service

49. Go to “System”-“Exit System” to exit the software

47. wait for the turbo to drop 0 rpm

50. Push the red button to turn off power, it also turn off the small chiller

51. press “STOP” button

52. turn off both Compressor and N2 Valves (picture showing is on)

Back room

54. turn off “COOLING”

55. turn off “MAINS”

53. rotate the Valve to the right to close it
